champion.narrative: No champion exists. The sole contact attempt was Jonathan Young, who was scheduled for a discovery call on 07 Aug 2025, no-showed, and has been unresponsive for 335 days. His role, title, and position within The Boston Beer Company are unknown—he does not appear in OpportunityContactRole, and the task log carries only his name with no additional context. We have no evidence that Jonathan ever advocated for Zycus, had access to budget authority or decision-makers, or drove any internal agenda. With zero follow-up, zero alternate contacts, and zero calls, this deal has no internal advocate and no multi-threaded relationships. A champion is existentially required to navigate a $100K CLM+AI evaluation at an enterprise buyer: someone in procurement, legal ops, or supply chain who will sell Zycus internally, introduce us to the Economic Buyer, and push back on competitors. The immediate move is to attempt re-engagement with Jonathan (one final outreach asking if he is still the right contact or if we should connect with someone else in procurement/legal), and in parallel research The Boston Beer Company's procurement and legal leadership on LinkedIn to identify and secure an executive-connect introduction to a VP-level champion via the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley).

The Salesforce Competitors__c field is null (ground truth), no Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, or How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c fields exist in the org, zero buyer calls have occurred to surface competitive benchmarking, and no tasks or next-step history reference rival vendors. We do not know whether The Boston Beer Company is sole-sourcing Zycus, running a formal RFP against Icertis, Agiloft, or CLM incumbents, or simply exploring CLM+AI without urgency. For a $100K enterprise CLM deal, competitive context is critical: if we are the only vendor engaged, we control the timeline and narrative; if we are in a bake-off against Icertis (strong in beverage/manufacturing) or Agiloft (flexible workflow engine), we must lead with Merlin's AI differentiation (negotiation intelligence on supplier pricing, agentic contract review) as the wedge. The move is to ask directly in discovery: 'Are you evaluating other CLM vendors alongside Zycus, and if so, who are you looking at and what are your early impressions?' Then position Merlin as the AI-native differentiator and make it the Phase 2 centerpiece of the evaluation.

items.action: Multi-thread the buying committee: Use the discovery call and any executive introduction to identify and engage 3-5 additional stakeholders across procurement, legal ops, supply chain, finance, and IT (who will own InfoSec review and ERP/systems integration for iContract+Merlin). items.action: Lead with Merlin AI differentiation in discovery and demo: Demonstrate Merlin's negotiation agent on supplier price history (show how it benchmarks The Boston Beer Company's ingredient/packaging supplier pricing against market data to drive savings), agentic contract review (automate redline analysis and obligation tracking to cut legal review cycles), and AI-driven compliance monitoring. Close date 30 Dec 2026 is 187 days out, but the deal is in Qualified stage with zero discovery completed and has been dark for 323 days. The enterprise CLM motion averages 12-15 months from discovery through close. Even with immediate restart, the close date is unachievable without compressing discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, vendor selection, commercials, contracting, and legal/InfoSec review into under 6 months, which is unrealistic for a $100K enterprise deal.

The intended primary contact Jonathan Young has been unresponsive for 323 days, signaling either deprioritization, internal political shift, or handoff to another stakeholder. Without a champion or an alternative engaged stakeholder, the deal has no internal advocate and will remain stalled.

RESTART OR DISQUALIFY WITHIN 7 DAYS: 323 days dark, zero discovery completed, 187 days to a Dec 2026 close that requires 12-15 months of enterprise motion. The deal owner must decide by 3 Jul 2026 whether to mount an executive-led restart (owner's manager Alexa Bradley engaging senior procurement leadership to validate need and secure Jonathan Young or an alternative champion) or disqualify and remove from pipeline. A third option (continue to drift) guarantees failure.

EXECUTIVE MULTI-THREAD IF RESTARTING: If the decision is to restart, the owner's manager Alexa Bradley should engage senior procurement leadership (the account has a Senior Executive Procurement Leader, Chief Supply Chain Officer, and SVP Supply Chain and Quality on record) to validate whether a CLM + Merlin initiative is active, who owns it, and whether Jonathan Young is the right contact or a different stakeholder should lead. An exec-to-exec conversation will uncover whether this opportunity is real or whether it should be closed lost.

BUILD CHAMPION FROM ZERO: Jonathan Young was a no-show 7 Aug 2025 and has not replied in 323 days, signaling disengagement or deprioritization. If he re-engages, assess his access to budget authority and advocacy immediately. If he remains unresponsive, identify an alternative champion among the senior procurement contacts and build advocacy from scratch. Without a champion by the second call, this deal will stall again.

value.detail: Close date 30 Dec 2026 is 175 days out, but deal is in Qualified stage with zero discovery completed and has been dark for 335 days. Enterprise CLM motion averages 12-15 months (365-457 days) from discovery through RFI/RFP, vendor selection, workshops, commercials, ROI, contracting, InfoSec, and legal. To close by 30 Dec requires compressing the entire cycle into under 6 months (175 days), which is 50-62% below realistic pace for a $100K CLM+Merlin deal. Even with immediate restart and executive sponsorship, the close date is unachievable without bypassing standard enterprise gates (RFP, workshops, multi-round negotiation, InfoSec review), which is unrealistic for a mid-to-large buyer like The Boston Beer Company. Forward slip risk is 100%.

gaps: No buyer calls discovered via Avoma opp or account association (zero matched meetings after opp+account discovery and attendee matching against bostonbeer.com domain and known contacts Jonathan Young, Brian Henrici). This is a genuine knowledge gap: no voice-of-customer data exists.

No MEDDPICC synthesis-map fields exist in org schema: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c, Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c all returned INVALID_FIELD on Q3 SOQL. This is a schema limitation, not a data-entry hygiene gap. However, the underlying MEDDPICC knowledge is also genuinely missing because zero discovery calls occurred, so this compounds the knowledge gap.

Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c are null: no submission-date milestones recorded. This is a hygiene gap (the deal has not progressed past Qualified, so no milestone dates exist), but it also reflects a genuine knowledge gap (no progression = no time-in-stage math beyond creation-to-now).

Current_Contract_Expiration__c does not exist in org schema (INVALID_FIELD): cannot assess whether a contract renewal forcing function exists. This is a schema limitation, but also a knowledge gap because zero discovery calls occurred to surface whether The Boston Beer Company has an incumbent CLM vendor or contract expiration timeline.

AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are null: no AI excitement score or rationale documented. Product scope includes Merlin, suggesting AI appetite, but AIS is unscored. This is a hygiene gap (AIS fields exist but are unpopulated), compounded by a knowledge gap (zero calls to validate AI appetite).

Zero contact roles (OpportunityContactRole query returned zero records): no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, Champion, or any stakeholder mapped. This is both a hygiene gap (roles should be added as contacts are engaged) and a knowledge gap (only one contact attempted, never engaged, so buying committee is genuinely unknown).
